items.title: The Millionaire Real Estate Agent (revised insights, widely read 2016–2025)
items.author: Gary Keller
items.summary: A models-based playbook arguing that lead generation, budgeting, and hiring discipline — not luck — drive results. Most relevant to sellers choosing an agent: it explains the systems behind consistent marketing and pricing.

items.title: Your First Home
items.author: Gary Keller, Jay Papasan
items.summary: A step-by-step guide for first-time buyers covering pre-approval, agent selection, offers, and closing. Strong on the emotional and financial readiness questions South Bay buyers face.

items.title: Home Buying Kit For Dummies
items.author: Eric Tyson, Ray Brown
items.summary: A comprehensive, jargon-free reference on mortgages, inspections, and negotiation. Useful for understanding loan trade-offs when rates are elevated.

items.title: The Book on Negotiating Real Estate
items.author: J Scott, Mark Ferguson, Carol Scott
items.summary: Tactics for structuring offers and counteroffers in competitive markets — directly applicable to multiple-offer South Bay scenarios on both the buy and sell side.

items.title: Zillow Talk: Rewriting the Rules of Real Estate
items.author: Spencer Rascoff, Stan Humphries
items.summary: Data-driven myth-busting on pricing, timing, and renovations that pay off. A good primer on how listing strategy and seasonality affect sale price.
